methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The Boring Company is a late-stage Transportation company currently valued at $5.7B post-money, having raised $675M to date. The private peer set in Transportation consists of four companies including Rivian, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft; no public comparables exist in the narrow peer universe, limiting traditional multiples-based approaches.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, the only triangulation method with sufficient data integrity. Using the company's $5.7B last post-money valuation as a base and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage advancement, this method implies a valuation of $6.8B. The peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable companies within Transportation—while four sector peers exist, none met the density threshold of three or more datapoints in filtered cohorts.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.8B to $7.9B, with a median implied value of $6.8B. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological anchor rather than true triangulation. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market benchmarks for stage-progression rates in specialized transportation infrastructure, the heterogeneity of the private peer set, and limited visibility into near-term financing activity that would establish fresher market signals. Materiality of future funding rounds and market conditions in deep-infrastructure capital deployment remain primary drivers of valuation revision.
